title: quercus robur subsp. sessiliflora
intro: Quercus robur subsp. sessiliflora, also known as the pedunculate oak, is a subspecies of the pedunculate oak tree (Quercus robur) native to Europe and parts of Asia. This tree has been used in traditional medicine for centuries due to its rich content of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other bioactive compounds, which exhibit anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The medicinal properties of Quercus robur subsp. sessiliflora have been found to provide various health benefits, including reducing cholesterol levels, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting the immune system. The herbal preparations of this tree include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of ailments, from digestive issues to respiratory problems.
